nodes.title: Clarify how the `--events` argument works
nodes.body: This change updates the documentation for the `--events` argument to clarify how you can pass a list of multiple events you want to listen for.

nodes.title: Allow the user to configure the path where webhooks are forwarded
nodes.body:
This allows users to configure `gh webhook forward` to send their webhooks to a path other than `/` on their chosen `localhost` port. For example, you might locate your webhook handler at `/github`.

I use the slightly awkwardly-named `--delivery-path`/`-D` argument because the natural letter, `P`, is already taken by `--part`.

Nit: No need to specify type twice if all consecutive arguments share the same type. 

```suggestion
func runFwd(out io.Writer, port int, path, token, wsURL string, activateHook func() error) error {
```

Ensures there's always a slash at the beginning of path: 

```suggestion
	webhookRcvServerURL := fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d/%s", port, strings.TrimPrefix(path, "/"))
```

nodes.title: Change `--host` argument to `--github-host`
nodes.body: This renames the `--host` argument to `--github-host` to make it clearer what exactly it does. We keep the `-H` alias, as that still feels natural.

nodes.title: Swap the `--port` argument for a `--url` argument, giving the user more control
nodes.body: This replaces the `--port` argument with a new, more powerful `--url` argument, allowing the user to control the protocol, host, path *and* port through a single CLI argument.

Small nit for Go convention:

```suggestion
	URL        string
```
Probs need to say github-host now that the other pr is merged.
I think at this point we can totally remove the "If the --host flag is not specified..." sentence because it's really not for customers but for us developers of the extension.
Remove port?

nodes.title: Document how to forward all events
nodes.body: It took me a couple minutes to find how to use the equivalent of the "Send me everything." option from the website, so I figured it might help others to include that in the help page.

nodes.title: Move hook creation to backend
nodes.body: This PR moves the createHook function to the backend so that the websocket server can manage the entire lifecycle of cli-hooks.

nodes.title: Update to latest release of `go-gh`
nodes.body:
### Token storage change in latest release of `go-gh`

Recent release of [`gh`](https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/latest) and [`go-gh`](https://github.com/cli/go-gh/releases/latest) contain changes which affected this extension.

The latest release introduced the feature of storing authentication tokens in the system keyring (encrypted storage) instead of in a plain text file.
The keyrings that are supported are:

- Keychain on macOS
- GNOME Keyring on Linux (Secret Service dbus interface)
- Wincred on Windows

This has huge security benefits for the users of the tool and was one of our oldest outstanding [issues](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/449). Unfortunately this change has broken this extension.

### Changes in this PR

- Upgrade the used `go-gh` version to v1.2.1, the latest version.

nodes.title: Fix fetching auth token from gh
nodes.body:
Fixes https://github.com/cli/gh-webhook/issues/11
Closes https://github.com/cli/gh-webhook/pull/9

nodes.title: Restore forward compatibility with non-github.com hosts
nodes.body: Reverts #8 to switch back to an approach where the websockets URL is obtained from the GitHub API response rather than being hardcoded to `wss://webhook-forwarder.github.com`. In the future, other GitHub hosts might support this feature, and when they do, this extension will be compatible.

nodes.title: Bump golang.org/x/net from 0.7.0 to 0.17.0
nodes.body:
Bumps [golang.org/x/net](https://github.com/golang/net) from 0.7.0 to 0.17.0.
Commits
  • b225e7c http2: limit maximum handler goroutines to MaxConcurrentStreams
  • 88194ad go.mod: update golang.org/x dependencies
  • 2b60a61 quic: fix several bugs in flow control accounting
  • 73d82ef quic: handle DATA_BLOCKED frames
  • 5d5a036 quic: handle streams moving from the data queue to the meta queue
  • 350aad2 quic: correctly extend peer's flow control window after MAX_DATA
  • 21814e7 quic: validate connection id transport parameters
  • a600b35 quic: avoid redundant MAX_DATA updates
  • ea63359 http2: check stream body is present on read timeout
  • ddd8598 quic: version negotiation

nodes.title: Update dependencies and Go to version 1.21
nodes.body:
This PR does three things:
* Updates Go from 1.19 to 1.21
* Updates dependencies to address dependabot security alert, superseding https://github.com/cli/gh-webhook/pull/26
* Removes the unused `golang.org/x/crypto` statement in `go.mod`

nodes.body:
Bumps [github.com/cli/go-gh/v2](https://github.com/cli/go-gh) from 2.4.0 to 2.11.1.

Security

    For more information, see https://github.com/cli/go-gh/security/advisories/GHSA-55v3-xh23-96gh

nodes.title: Bump github.com/cli/go-gh/v2 from 2.11.1 to 2.12.1
nodes.body:
Bumps [github.com/cli/go-gh/v2](https://github.com/cli/go-gh) from 2.11.1 to 2.12.1.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/cli/go-gh/v2's releases.

v2.12.1

Security

A security vulnerability has been identified in go-gh where an attacker-controlled GitHub Enterprise Server could result in executing arbitrary commands on a user's machine by replacing HTTP URLs provided by GitHub with local file paths for browsing.

For more information, see GHSA-g9f5-x53j-h563

Full Changelog: https://github.com/cli/go-gh/compare/v2.12.0...v2.12.1

v2.12.0

Introducing experimental support for rendering markdown with customizable, accessible colors

Users with low vision or color blindness rely upon the terminal's ability to change how colors appear, however this requires CLIs to use a limited set of colors:

The markdown rendered for GitHub CLI and extensions defaults to 8-bit colors that users cannot easily customize in this way.

Now, users can force rendered markdown to use customizable, accessible colors by doing one of the following:

  1. Set GH_ACCESSIBLE_COLORS environment variable to a truthy value

    export GH_ACCESSIBLE_COLORS=1

  2. Set accessible_colors configuration setting

    gh config set accessible_colors enabled

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ccf829c5-6987-453b-a72a-51f452a80d26

Users with a customcharmbracelet/glamour style will continue to have markdown rendered using it.

For more information, see cli/go-gh#186

Introducing new experimental github.com/cli/go-gh/v2/pkg/x package

cli/go-gh provides capabilities used by both the GitHub CLI and CLI extensions. Some of these packages are stable, however some like the new experimental markdown support are not.

The github.com/cli/go-gh/v2/pkg/x package has been created to contain experimental features that are subject to change without notice.

Introducing new string matcher functions for Go templates

The following string matcher functions from Masterminds/sprig have been incorporated for more robust Go template support in GitHub CLI and CLI extensions:

  • contains
  • hasPrefix

... (truncated)

Commits

  • 1ecf6c4 Expand test cases for schem case sensitivity
  • 56c6f10 Parse and examine URL, assume scheme if missing

  • df956a6 Initial fix for browsing URLs
  • 61bf393 Merge pull request #186 from cli/accessible-colors
  • 024a89f Remove toolchain directive from library
  • 09a555f Disable table wrapping for all
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view
